steps.title: Identify the reversal signaled by 'seemingly'
steps.content: The eruption seems inhospitable to life, but Boyd's quote will show it's actually "a cradle for biodiversity"—opposite claims. The word 'seemingly' flags that appearance contradicts reality.

steps.title: Apply the concessive transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is concession. The geyser appears inhospitable ("seemingly"), but it is actually a cradle for biodiversity — appearance contradicts reality.
  • Choice A — Thus,: signals that biodiversity follows as a result of the hostile conditions — the opposite of the intended meaning, so fails. ✗
  • Choice B — Still,: signals "despite the apparent hostility, here is what is actually true" — bridges the contradiction, so fits. ✓
  • Choice C — Specifically,: signals narrowing into a specific instance of the previous claim, but the second sentence contradicts rather than specifies the first, so fails. ✗
  • Choice D — In other words,: signals restatement, but hostile conditions and a cradle for biodiversity are opposites, not paraphrases, so fails. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
